reasoning: I vote for Shantanu Thakur without doubt. He is from our Matua community and he fought for the CAA in Parliament when no one else did—my grandmother waited seventy years for citizenship papers, and now she has them because of what he did. Mamata gave us the ration card and Kanyashree stipend, and I am grateful for that, truly, but gratitude is not the same as a fundamental right. Shantanu kept his word. Biswajit Das switched parties like he didn't know what he believed; Mamata's government protected Shantanu Misra for 55 days while women in Sandeshkhali suffered. The BJP promised Didu her citizenship and delivered it. I cannot vote for anything less than that.
primary_drivers: The CAA and Didu's citizenship papers—permanent structural change that only the BJP delivered
Shantanu Thakur's loyalty to the community and his role in Parliament fighting for the CAA
Loss of trust in Mamata's governance after Sandeshkhali, SSC corruption, and broken promises
